_comment_allow: Pre-approve the dev workflow and GitHub read operations. .venv/bin/* covers any venv binary (pytest, ruff, mypy, pyright, etc.). make * covers all Makefile targets. gh read commands are non-mutating — no confirmation needed. gh api* is intentionally broad: it is used almost exclusively for GET reads, and the write operations that matter (issues, PRs, comments) have explicit ask rules below.
_comment_deny: git staging is never permitted — only the developer stages files (CLAUDE.md rule 4). test-ha-config/.storage/** contains live modem credentials in plaintext; reading it leaks passwords into the conversation context (CLAUDE.md rule 28 covers Bash readers; this is the hard backstop for the Read tool).
_comment_ask: Any git operation that changes history or pushes to a remote requires explicit per-action confirmation. gh write operations are enumerated explicitly so reads (view, list, checks, diff, api) are never caught by a broad ask rule — ask beats allow when both match.
